method_atom: A universal construction that takes any finite-dimensional algebraic structure specified purely by its structure constants and represents its infinitesimal generators as formal power series in a formal deformation parameter, with coefficients living in an associative operator algebra generated by coordinates and their derivatives. The series coefficients are obtained in closed form from the structure constants alone via combinatorial identities involving Bernoulli numbers and successive derivatives of a fixed generating function, and the same object is reached by three interchangeable routes: direct multilinear (tensor) calculation, a geometric computation of invariant vector fields in adapted coordinates, and a coalgebraic argument using coderivations and bialgebra structure.
object_atom: n-dimensional Lie algebras g over a field or ring containing the rationals, equipped with a chosen vector-space basis, and their concrete realization inside the Weyl algebra A_n (together with the associated formal group scheme and Hopf-algebra description of g).
brief_reason: The transferable part is the structure-constant-only recipe mapping abstract generators to power series with operator-algebra coefficients plus the Bernoulli/generating-function combinatorics that computes them; the domain-specific part is Lie algebras and their embedding in the Weyl algebra.
